top_signals.title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 6/23/2026
top_signals.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Apollo curbs withdrawals after exit requests hit 17%, reigniting fears over private credit liquidity
top_signals.summary: Apollo is capping investor redemptions in its main retail-focused private credit fund after exit requests hit 17%.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Brexit 10 years on: Business does not want a referendum rerun, says CBI chief
top_signals.summary: British businesses are focused on the future and do not want a rerun of the Brexit referendum, the leader of the UK’s largest business lobby group has warned. Speaking to the FT, CBI director-general Rain Newton-Smith, on the tenth anniversary of the referendum, stated that despite the economic pain of leaving the EU, businesses don’t [...]

top_signals.title: StubHub UK ordered to pay £1.5mn penalty for ‘drip pricing’
top_signals.summary: Ticket seller fined by competition watchdog for failing to include additional charges in prices advertised to customers

top_signals.title: German state election draws Bauhaus into AfD culture war - Reuters
top_signals.summary: German state election draws Bauhaus into AfD culture war  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Korean Stocks Tumble 10% as Soaring Volatility Rattles Investors
top_signals.summary: South Korean stocks tumbled from a record high as investors sold off chip heavyweights, delivering another outsized swing that has become a feature of the world’s best‑performing market this year.

top_signals.title: Permian natural gas production increased faster than crude oil
top_signals.summary: The Permian region's marketed natural gas production grew from 17.2 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) in 2021 to 27.6 Bcf/d in 2025, a 60% increase, according to data from our latest Short Term Energy Outlook. Over the same period, crude oil production grew by 39%, going from 4.7 million barrels per day (b/d) to 6.6 million b/d. The higher growth in natural gas production is the result of increasing gas-oil ratios (GOR).
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Meta taps Indian startup founder to solve WhatsApp's revenue puzzle
Royal Mail earnings jump despite employment cost hikes
top_signals.summary: Royal Mail has seen its revenue jump as it shook off rising employment costs and declining letter volumes.  The postal service saw revenue climb 2.6 per cent to £8.4bn in the year to March, as operating profit remained “broadly flat” at £5m.  Royal Mail said this growth came despite “the impact of national insurance increases” [...]
